max_model_len_provenance: HF config text_config.max_position_embeddings = 262144 (256K). Equals canvas_length(256) * max_blocks(1024). This is the capacity limit for prompt + generated, NOT a requirement that prompts be 262144 tokens (HF DiffusionGemmaGenerationMixin.generate computes max_length = input_len + max_new_tokens).

max_model_len_physical_limit_evidence.device: QB2 / bh-qbge-06 / P150x4 (2 Blackhole PCIe cards, 4 Tensix, TP=4, (1,4) mesh)
max_model_len_physical_limit_evidence.fit: Full 30-layer 26B-A4B build + 256K KV caches allocate on the (1,4) mesh: post-build DRAM 29.704 GiB/chip used, 2.163 GiB/chip free (plan.md 2026-07-02 full-depth 256K probe).
max_model_len_physical_limit_evidence.sampler_constraint: Full-vocab (262144) on-device Gumbel/noise materialization OOMs in the fragmented DRAM remaining after 256K-KV allocation (ttnn.rand asks for ~1 GiB; largest free block ~101 MiB/bank). The chunked/no-materialize Gumbel sampler (tt/sampling.py::gumbel_max_with_chunked_noise, --chunked-gumbel-sampling) fits full-depth 256K + canvas_length=256: DG_TEXT_DEMO_SUCCESS at 29.704 GiB/chip used.
max_model_len_physical_limit_evidence.verdict: 262144 is the advertised and servable context. No reduction below the HF-advertised context. Serving must use the chunked-Gumbel sampler (or argmax RUN-first sampler) at full 256K to stay within DRAM.

output_block_granularity.note: DiffusionGemma emits a 256-token BLOCK per decode step (one denoised+committed canvas), not one token. This is the OUTPUT block granularity; it is intrinsic to block-diffusion generation and is NOT an input-alignment constraint.
output_block_granularity.input_alignment: Prompt length may be any valid length up to max_model_len. There is no requirement that prompt length be divisible by 256, by a prefill tile (32), by block_size, or by a page. prefill_prompt_tokens pads the prompt to a 32-tile multiple internally and reports the logical/aligned lengths; the intrinsic 256-token output block granularity does not constrain the prompt length.

datatype_policy.source: Current full-model bring-up policy (create_tt_model default). The dg-07 datatype-sweep stage has NOT been run for DiffusionGemma; no reduced-precision selection has been recorded, so serving uses the full-model policy.
datatype_policy.ccl: ttnn.bfloat16 (backbone TP=4 all-reduce/all-gather via gemma4 CCL)
datatype_policy.self_conditioning: ttnn.bfloat16 tensors; softmax/soft-embedding accumulate with fp32 (WormholeComputeKernelConfig HiFi4, fp32_dest_acc_en=True) per denoise_forward.default_self_conditioning_compute_kernel_config
datatype_policy.matches_gemma4_vllm_bridge: Yes — models/demos/gemma4/tt/generator_vllm.py serves bfloat16 weights + bfloat16 KV cache. fp32 experts do not fit QB2 DRAM and fp32-faithful router top-k is blocked by ttnn.topk TT_FATAL on FLOAT32 (plan.md), so bf16/MoE is the fastest policy that fits.

mesh.max_num_seqs_note: Batch-1 single-user latency is the headline target. Larger batch/concurrency (up to 32) is bounded by the 256K-KV per-request footprint; the block-diffusion per-step canvas K/V scratch (#47474 storage class ii) is additional per-request memory. Record the largest tested max_num_seqs in the serving evidence.

sampling.mode: on-device canvas sampling (sample_on_device_mode=all). No host greedy/top-1 argmax path, no full-logits readback. Per-step decision tensors ([B,L] argmax/entropy/sampled/accept) are read back only for the data-dependent halt check; the full [B,L,vocab] logits stay on device.
sampling.procedure: Per denoise step: temperature-scale (linear 0.8->0.4) -> Gumbel-max argmax(logits/T + gumbel) -> entropy-budget acceptance (sort/cumsum/scatter) -> renoise rejected positions to random tokens. Commit = clean argmax. RUN-first: --argmax-sampling routes clean argmax (gumbel_noise=None) to avoid full-vocab noise allocation at 256K.
sampling.optimize_perf_47465: Terminal decision path is trace-safe and optimized (decisions unchanged): argmax over the 262144 vocab uses ROW_MAJOR multi-core (sampling.argmax_last_dim; bit-identical to TILE argmax, 1240ms->14.4ms/op, 2x/step); accept/renoise constants are preallocated once outside the trace (denoise_loop.make_denoise_constants: budget_t/accept_zeros/renoise_ones, a few KB persistent, replacing per-step ttnn.full/zeros_like host writes that are illegal during trace capture). Trace-safe fixed-step loop denoise_loop.run_fixed_denoise_steps (fixed <=48-step count, device canvas feedback, no host readback of the cutoff). Traced full 30-layer denoise step ~4176 ms; per block (48 steps + commit) ~232 s. No dtype/mesh/max_model_len change.
